issue.body:
Tracks: #18

Spec for validating the full feature-request lifecycle chain end-to-end on pd-book-tools:
triage → spec-from-issue → decompose-spec → ship-issue.

Tracks: #18

Open design questions:
- Does the label transition chain (triage:approved → triage:needs-spec → spec child → decompose milestone → child with bot:ship-issue-ready) work correctly?
- Are milestone creation and child-issue linking idempotent on re-run?
- Does ship-issue still claim a bot:ship-issue-ready child issued from this chain?

Spec: docs/specs/2026-05-10-feature-request-lifecycle-e2e-smoke-design.md

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #19

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#312

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/ocr/document.py:85`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:2685`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/block.py:1006`

`Document.scale()` omits `source_identifier`; `Page.scale()` omits metadata such as image path/name/source/rotation/review/provenance/failure fields; `Block.scale()` omits sort override, unmatched GT words, additional attributes, base GT text, and review.

Impact:

- Coordinate conversion changes more than coordinates.
- Consumers can lose provenance/review metadata unexpectedly.

Remediation:

- Implement scale via `to_dict()`/`from_dict()` style deep-copy transforms, replacing only dimensions and bboxes.
- Add metadata-preservation tests.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: `scale()` drops model metadata
